authors: Soulas T, Gurruchaga JM, Palfi S, Cesaro P, Nguyen JP, Fénelon G
title: Attempted and completed suicides after subthalamic nucleus stimulation for Parkinson's disease
study_question: What is the prevalence and characteristics of suicide in STN DBS patients with Parkinson's disease?
population_assessed: 200 Parkinson's disease patients undergoing bilateral subthalamic nucleus deep brain stimulation between 1997-2006
follow_up_duration: Mean 12.0 months after surgery; study period 1997-2006
outcome_measures: Completed suicide, attempted suicide, depression scores, cognitive status, impulse control
inclusion_criteria: Parkinson's disease patients undergoing bilateral STN DBS at single center; preoperative psychiatric assessment available
answer_to_study_question: Two patients (1%) completed suicide and four (2%) attempted suicide. Suicidal behavior associated with postoperative depression and altered impulse regulation.
